oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Case report: Tebupirimfos insecticide toxicosis in a cow-calf herd
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Radke, Scott L.
Magstadt, Drew R.
Burns, Laura
Schrunk, Dwayne
Bertram, Renee
Cordray, Aubrey
Harm, Tyler
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: bovine
tebupirimfos
organophosphate
poisoning
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Acute high morbidity and mortality in adult beef cows and nursing calves exposed to the organothiophosphate insecti­cide tebupirimfos through accidental incorporation into feed was investigated. The clinical signs of ataxia, recumbency, increased salivation, urination, defecation and severe respira­tory distress associated with a cholinesterase inhibitor were observed. Brain cholinesterase in both cows and calves was significantly depressed by 95-100%. Presence of tebupirimfos was detected in cow rumen content, milk and calf gastric con­tent through both gas chromatography-mass spectrometry and liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: The Bovine Practitioner; Vol. 60 No. 2 (2026): Online First; 18-20
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
